statements.name: MCHART
statements.description: creates a chart for subgroup medians, which is used to monitor the central tendency of a process
statements.help:
MCHART  (processes) * subgroup-variable<(block-variables)><=symbol-variable | ='character'></ options>;                                              
process is the name of the variable containing the raw measurements, or the common prefix of the summary variables in the HISTORY= data set, or the value of the variable _VAR_ in the TABLE= data set                     
subgroup-variable is the variable that identifies subgroups in the data                     
block-variables are optional variables that group the data into blocks of consecutive subgroups                     
symbol-variable is an optional variable whose levels (unique values) determine the symbol marker that is used to plot the medians                     
character is a character that is used to plot the medians when you produce traditional graphics

arguments.name: ALLLABEL=
arguments.description: labels every point on the primary chart with the value plotted for that subgroup or with the value of variable in the input data set

arguments.name: ALLLABEL2=
arguments.description: labels every point on an R, s, or trend chart with the value plotted for that subgroup or with the value of variable in the input data set

arguments.name: ALLN
arguments.description: plots summary statistics for all subgroups, regardless of whether the subgroup sample size equals the nominal control limit sample size n specified by the LIMITN= option or the variable _LIMITN_ in the LIMITS= data set

arguments.name: ALPHA=
arguments.description: requests α probability limits instead of 3σ control limits

arguments.name: BLOCKLABELPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the position of a block-variable label in the block legend

arguments.name: BLOCKLABTYPE=
arguments.description: specifies how lengthy block variable values are treated when there is insufficient space to display them in the block legend

arguments.name: BLOCKPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the vertical position of the legend for the values of the block-variables

arguments.name: BLOCKREP
arguments.description: specifies that block variable values for all subgroups are to be displayed

arguments.name: BLOCKVAR=
arguments.description: specifies variables whose values are used to assign colors for filling the background of the legend associated with block variables

arguments.name: BOXES=
arguments.description: specifies a variable whose values are used to assign colors for the outlines of box-and-whiskers plots

arguments.name: BOXFILL=
arguments.description: specifies how box-and-whisker plots are filled with colors from the ODS style

arguments.name: CFRAMELAB=
arguments.description: specifies the color for filling rectangles that frame the point labels displayed with the ALLLABEL=, ALLLABEL2=, OUTLABEL=, and OUTLABEL2= options

arguments.name: CIINDICES
arguments.description: requests capability index confidence limits based on subgroup summary data, calculated using "effective degrees of freedom"

arguments.name: TYPE=
arguments.description: specifies the type of confidence limit

arguments.name: ALPHA=
arguments.description: specifies the default confidence level to compute confidence limits

arguments.name: CINFILL=
arguments.description: specifies the color for the area inside the upper and lower control limits

arguments.name: CLIPFACTOR=
arguments.description: requests clipping of extreme points on the control chart

arguments.name: CLIPLEGEND=
arguments.description: specifies the label for the legend that indicates the number of clipped points when the CLIPFACTOR= option is used

arguments.name: CLIPSUBCHAR=
arguments.description: specifies a substitution character (such as #) for the label provided with the CLIPLEGEND= option

arguments.name: COUT
arguments.description: specifies the color for the plotting symbols and the portions of connecting line segments that lie outside the control limits

arguments.name: CSTAROUT
arguments.description: specifies a color for those portions of the outlines of stars (requested with the STARVERTICES= option) that exceed the inner or outer circles

arguments.name: DISCRETE
arguments.description: specifies that numeric subgroup variable values be treated as discrete values, so that each tick value on the default subgroup axis corresponds to a unique subgroup variable value

arguments.name: EXCHART
arguments.description: creates a control chart only when exceptions occur, specifically, when the control limits are exceeded or when any of the tests requested with the TESTS= option or the TESTS2= option are positive

arguments.name: FRONTREF
arguments.description: draws reference lines specified with the HREF= and VREF= options in front of box-and-whiskers plots

arguments.name: GRID
arguments.description: adds a grid to the control chart

arguments.name: HAXIS=
arguments.description: specifies tick mark values for the horizontal (subgroup) axis

arguments.name: HOFFSET=
arguments.description: specifies the length of the offset at each end of the horizontal axis

arguments.name: HREF=
arguments.description: draws reference lines perpendicular to the horizontal (subgroup) axis on the primary chart

arguments.name: HREF2=
arguments.description: draws reference lines perpendicular to the horizontal (subgroup) axis on the secondary chart

arguments.name: HREF2DATA=
arguments.description: draws reference lines perpendicular to the horizontal (subgroup) axis on the secondary chart

arguments.name: HREF2LABELS=
arguments.description: specifies labels for the reference lines requested by the HREF2= option

arguments.name: HREFDATA=
arguments.description: draws reference lines perpendicular to the horizontal (subgroup) axis on the primary chart

arguments.name: HREFLABELS=
arguments.description: specifies labels for the reference lines requested by the HREF= option

arguments.name: HREFLABPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the vertical position of the HREFLABEL= and HREF2LABEL= labels
positions along top of subplot area
staggers from top to bottom of subplot area
positions along bottom of subplot area
staggers from bottom to top of subplot area

arguments.name: INDEPENDENTZONES
arguments.description: specifies that the widths of the zones requested with the ZONES option be computed independently above and below the center line of the chart, so that the width of each zone is one-third of the difference between the process mean and the control limit on its side of the chart

arguments.name: INTERVAL=
arguments.description: specifies the natural time interval between consecutive subgroup positions when a time, date, or datetime format is associated with a numeric subgroup variable
arguments.help: INTERVAL=DAY | DTDAY | HOUR | MINUTE | MONTH | QTR | SECOND

arguments.name: INTSTART=
arguments.description: specifies the starting value for a numeric horizontal axis, when a date, time, or datetime format is associated with the subgroup variable

arguments.name: LCLLABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a label for the lower control limit in the primary chart

arguments.name: LIMITN=
arguments.description: specifies either a fixed or varying nominal sample size for the control limits

arguments.name: LIMLABSUBCHAR=
arguments.description: specifies a substitution character (such as #) for labels provided as quoted strings with the LCLLABEL=, LCLLABEL2=, UCLLABEL=, UCLLABEL2=, CSYMBOL=, NPSYMBOL=, PSYMBOL=, RSYMBOL=, SSYMBOL=, USYMBOL=, and XSYMBOL= options

arguments.name: LSL=
arguments.description: provides lower specification limits used to compute capability indices

arguments.name: MAXPANELS=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of pages or screens for a chart

arguments.name: MEDCENTRAL=
arguments.description: identifies a method for estimating the process mean μ, which is represented by the central line on a median chart

arguments.name: AVGMEAN
arguments.description: uses the average of subgroup means

arguments.name: AVGMED
arguments.description: uses the average of subgroup medians

arguments.name: MEDMED
arguments.description: uses the median of subgroup medians

arguments.name: MISSBREAK
arguments.description: determines how subgroups are formed when observations are read from a DATA= data set and a character subgroup-variable is provided

arguments.name: MU0=
arguments.description: specifies a known (standard) value μ0 for the process mean μ

arguments.name: NDECIMAL=
arguments.description: specifies the number of decimal digits in the default labels for the control limits and the central line in the primary chart

arguments.name: NEEDLES
arguments.description: connects plotted points to the central line with vertical line segments (needles)

arguments.name: NMARKERS
arguments.description: identifies a plotted subgroup summary statistic with a special symbol marker (character) when the corresponding subgroup sample size is not equal to the nominal control limit sample size n

arguments.name: NO3SIGMACHECK
arguments.description: suppresses the check for 3σ limits when tests for special causes are requested

arguments.name: NOBYREF
arguments.description: specifies that the reference line information in an HREF=, HREF2=, VREF=, or VREF2= data set is to be applied uniformly to charts created for all the BY groups in the input data set (DATA=, HISTORY=, or TABLE=)

arguments.name: NOCHART
arguments.description: suppresses the creation of the chart

arguments.name: NOCONNECT
arguments.description: suppresses line segments that connect points on the chart

arguments.name: NOCTL
arguments.description: suppresses the display of the central line in a primary chart

arguments.name: NOHLABEL
arguments.description: suppresses the label for the horizontal (subgroup) axis

arguments.name: NOLCL
arguments.description: suppresses the display of the lower control limit in a primary chart

arguments.name: NOLEGEND
arguments.description: suppresses the default legend for subgroup sample sizes, which appears by default below the chart

arguments.name: NOLIMITLABEL
arguments.description: suppresses the default labels for the control limits and central lines

arguments.name: NOLIMITS
arguments.description: suppresses the display of control limits

arguments.name: NOLIMITSLEGEND
arguments.description: suppresses the legend for the control limits, which appears by default in the upper right corner of the chart

arguments.name: NOOVERLAYLEGEND
arguments.description: suppresses the legend for overlay variables which is displayed by default when the OVERLAY= or OVERLAY2= option is specified

arguments.name: NOREADLIMITS
arguments.description: specifies that the control limits for each process listed in the chart statement not be read from the LIMITS= data set specified in the PROC SHEWHART statement

arguments.name: NOTESTACROSS
arguments.description: specifies that tests for special causes requested with the TESTS= or TESTS2= options not be applied across the boundaries of phases (blocks of consecutive subgroups) determined by the READPHASES= option and the variable _PHASE_ in the input data set

arguments.name: NOTICKREP
arguments.description: applies to character-valued subgroup-variables and specifies that only the first occurrence of repeated, adjacent subgroup values is to be labeled on the horizontal axis

arguments.name: NOTRENDCONNECT
arguments.description: suppresses line segments that connect points on a trend chart when the TRENDVAR= option is used

arguments.name: NOUCL
arguments.description: suppresses the display of the upper control limit in a primary chart

arguments.name: NPANELPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the number of subgroup positions per panel on each chart

arguments.name: OUTFILL
arguments.description: fills the areas outside the control limits that lie between the connected points and the control limits and are bounded by connecting lines

arguments.name: OUTHISTORY=
arguments.description: creates an output data set that contains the subgroup summary statistics

arguments.name: OUTINDEX=
arguments.description: specifies the value of the _INDEX_ variable in the OUTLIMITS= output data set

arguments.name: OUTLABEL=
arguments.description: labels each point that falls outside the control limits on the primary chart with the value plotted for that subgroup or with the value of variable in the input data set

arguments.name: OUTLIMITS=
arguments.description: creates an output data set that saves the control limits

arguments.name: OUTPHASE=
arguments.description: specifies the value of the _PHASE_ variable in the OUTHISTORY= data set

arguments.name: OUTTABLE=
arguments.description: creates an output SAS data set that saves the information plotted on the chart, including the subgroup variable values and their corresponding summary statistics and control limits

arguments.name: OVERLAY=
arguments.description: specifies variables to be overlaid on the primary control chart

arguments.name: OVERLAY2=
arguments.description: specifies variables to be overlaid on a secondary control chart

arguments.name: OVERLAY2ID=
arguments.description: specifies variables whose formatted values are used to label points on secondary chart overlays

arguments.name: OVERLAYID=
arguments.description: specifies variables whose formatted values are used to label points on primary chart overlays

arguments.name: OVERLAYLEGLAB=
arguments.description: specifies the label displayed to the left of the legend for overlays requested with the OVERLAY= or OVERLAY2= option

arguments.name: PAGENUM=
arguments.description: specifies the form of the label used for pagination; for example, 'Page #' or 'Page # of #'

arguments.name: PAGENUMPOS=
arguments.description: specifies where to position the page number requested with the PAGENUM= option
arguments.help: PAGENUMPOS=TL | TR | BL | BR | TL100 | TR100 | BL0 | BR0

arguments.name: TL
arguments.description: specifies the top left

arguments.name: TR
arguments.description: specifies the top right

arguments.name: BL
arguments.description: specifies the bottom left

arguments.name: BR
arguments.description: specifies the bottom right

arguments.name: TL100
arguments.description: specifies the very top left

arguments.name: TR100
arguments.description: specifies the very top right

arguments.name: BL0
arguments.description: specifies the very bottom left

arguments.name: BR0
arguments.description: specifies the very bottom right

arguments.name: PHASEBREAK
arguments.description: specifies that the last point in a phase (defined as a block of consecutive subgroups with the same value of the _PHASE_ variable) is not to be connected to the first point in the next phase

arguments.name: PHASELABTYPE=
arguments.description: specifies how lengthy _PHASE_ variable values are displayed when there is insufficient space in the legend requested with the PHASELEGEND option

arguments.name: PHASELEGEND
arguments.description: identifies the phases requested with the READPHASES= option in a legend across the top of the chart

arguments.name: PHASELIMITS
arguments.description: specifies that the control limits and center line be labeled for each phase specified with the READPHASES= option, providing the limits are constant within that phase

arguments.name: PHASEREF
arguments.description: delineates the phases specified with the READPHASES= option with reference lines drawn vertically

arguments.name: PHASEVARLABEL
arguments.description: displays the label associated with the variable _PHASE_ above the phase values in the phase legend

arguments.name: PHASEVALSEP
arguments.description: displays vertical lines separating phase values in the phase legend

arguments.name: RANGES
arguments.description: estimates the process standard deviation for a boxplot using subgroup ranges

arguments.name: READALPHA
arguments.description: specifies that the variable _ALPHA_, rather than the variable _SIGMAS_, be read from a LIMITS= data set when both variables are available in the data set

arguments.name: READINDEX=
arguments.description: reads one or more sets of control limits from a LIMITS= data set (specified in the PROC SHEWHART statement) for each process listed in the chart statement

arguments.name: READPHASES=
arguments.description: selects blocks of consecutive observations to be read from the input data set

arguments.name: REPEAT
arguments.description: specifies that the horizontal axis of a chart that spans multiple pages be arranged so that the last subgroup position on a page is repeated as the first subgroup position on the next page

arguments.name: RSYMBOL=
arguments.description: specifies a label for the central line in an R chart

arguments.name: R
arguments.description: R

arguments.name: RBAR
arguments.description: R bar

arguments.name: RPM
arguments.description: R prime

arguments.name: R0
arguments.description: R sub 0

arguments.name: SIGMA0=
arguments.description: specifies a known (standard) value σ0 for the process standard deviation σ

arguments.name: SIGMAS=
arguments.description: specifies the width of the control limits in terms of the multiple k of the standard error of the median statistic plotted on the chart

arguments.name: SKIPHLABELS=
arguments.description: specifies the number n of consecutive tick mark labels, beginning with the second tick mark label, that are thinned (not displayed) on the horizontal (subgroup) axis

arguments.name: SMETHOD=
arguments.description: specifies a method for estimating the process standard deviation, σ

arguments.name: NOWEIGHT
arguments.description: estimates σ as an unweighted average of unbiased subgroup estimates of σ

arguments.name: MVLUE
arguments.description: calculates a minimum variance linear unbiased estimate for σ

arguments.name: RMSDF
arguments.description: calculates a root-mean square estimate for σ

arguments.name: SPLIT=
arguments.description: specifies a special character that is inserted into the label of a process variable or summary statistic variable and whose purpose is to split the label into two parts

arguments.name: STARBDRADIUS=
arguments.description: specifies the radius of an imaginary circle that is the outer bound for vertices of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARCIRCLES=
arguments.description: specifies reference circles that are superimposed on the stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARINRADIUS=
arguments.description: specifies the inner radius of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARFILL=
arguments.description: specifies colors for filling the interior of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARLABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a method for labeling the vertices of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: ALL
arguments.description: labels all vertices of all stars

arguments.name: FIRST
arguments.description: labels all vertices of the leftmost star

arguments.name: HIGH
arguments.description: labels only vertices that lie outside the outer circle

arguments.name: LOW
arguments.description: labels only vertices that lie inside the inner circle

arguments.name: OUT
arguments.description: labels only vertices that lie inside the inner circle or outside the outer circle

arguments.name: STARLEGEND=
arguments.description: specifies the style of the legend used to identify the vertices of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: CLOCK
arguments.description: identifies the vertex variables by their positions on the clock (starting with 12:00)

arguments.name: CLOCK0
arguments.description: identifies the vertex variables by their positions on the clock (starting with 0:00)

arguments.name: DEGREES
arguments.description: identifies the vertex variables by angles in degrees, with 0 degrees corresponding to 12 o'clock

arguments.name: NONE
arguments.description: suppresses the legend

arguments.name: STARLEGENDLAB=
arguments.description: specifies the label displayed to the left of the legend for stars requested with the STARLEGEND= option

arguments.name: STARS=
arguments.description: specifies colors for the outlines of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARSPECS=
arguments.description: specifies the method used to standardize the star vertex variables listed with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARSTART=
arguments.description: specifies the vertex angle for the first variable in the STARVERTICES= list

arguments.name: STARTYPE=
arguments.description: specifies the style of the stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: CORONA
arguments.description: specifies a polygon with star-vertices emanating from the inner circle

arguments.name: POLYGON
arguments.description: specifies a closed polygon

arguments.name: RADIAL
arguments.description: draws rays emanating from the center

arguments.name: SPOKE
arguments.description: draws rays emanating from the inner circle

arguments.name: WEDGE
arguments.description: specifies a closed polygon with rays from the center to each vertex

arguments.name: STARVERTICES=
arguments.description: superimposes a star (polygon) at each point on the primary chart

arguments.name: STDDEVIATIONS
arguments.description: specifies that the estimate of the process standard deviation σ is to be calculated from subgroup standard deviations

arguments.name: SYMBOLLEGEND=
arguments.description: controls the legend for the levels of a symbol-variable

arguments.name: SYMBOLORDER=
arguments.description: specifies the order in which symbols are assigned for levels of symbol-variable

arguments.name: TABLES
arguments.description: creates a basic table of the subgroup values, the subgroup sample sizes, the subgroup summary statistics, and the upper and lower control limits

arguments.name: TABLEALL
arguments.description: tabulates all the information about the control chart

arguments.name: TABLECENTRAL
arguments.description: augments the basic table created by the TABLES option with columns for the values of the central lines

arguments.name: TABLEID
arguments.description: augments the basic table created by the TABLES option with a column for each of the ID variables

arguments.name: TABLELEGEND
arguments.description: adds a legend to the basic table created by the TABLE option

arguments.name: TABLEOUTLIM
arguments.description: augments the basic table created by the TABLE option with columns indicating which control limits (if any) are exceeded

arguments.name: TABLETESTS
arguments.description: augments the basic table created by the TABLES option with a column that indicates which of the tests for special causes (requested with the TESTS= option) are positive

arguments.name: TARGET=
arguments.description: provides target values used to compute the capability index Cpm, which is saved in the OUTLIMITS= data set

arguments.name: TEST2RESET=
arguments.description: enables tests for special causes to be reset in a secondary chart

arguments.name: TEST2RUN=
arguments.description: specifies the length of the pattern for Test 2 requested with the TESTS= and TESTS2= options
specifies a run-length of 7 in a row on one side of the central line
specifies a run-length of 8 in a row on one side of the central line
specifies a run-length of 9 in a row on one side of the central line
specifies at least 10 out of 11 in a row on one side of the central line
specifies at least 12 out of 14 in a row on one side of the central line
specifies at least 16 out of 20 in a row on one side of the central line

arguments.name: TEST3RUN=
arguments.description: specifies the length of the pattern for Test 3 requested with the TESTS= and TESTS2= options
searches for a pattern of steadily increasing or decreasing values with length at least 6
searches for a pattern of steadily increasing or decreasing values with length at least 7
searches for a pattern of steadily increasing or decreasing values with length at least 8

arguments.name: TESTACROSS
arguments.description: specifies that tests for special causes requested with the TESTS= or TESTS2= options be applied without regard to phases (blocks of consecutive subgroups) determined by the READPHASES= option and the variable _PHASE_ in the input data set

arguments.name: TESTLABBOX
arguments.description: requests that labels for subgroups with positive tests for special causes are positioned so they do not overlap

arguments.name: TESTLABEL=
arguments.description: provides labels for points at which one of the tests for special causes (requested with the TESTS= or TESTS2= option) is positive

arguments.name: TESTLABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a label for points at which the test for special causes requested with the index n in a TESTS= or TESTS2= list is positive

arguments.name: TESTNMETHOD=
arguments.description: applies the tests for special causes requested with the TESTS= and TESTS2= options to standardized test statistics when the subgroup sample sizes are not constant

arguments.name: TESTOVERLAP
arguments.description: applies tests for special causes (requested with the TESTS= or TESTS2= option) to overlapping patterns of points

arguments.name: TESTRESET=
arguments.description: enables tests for special causes to be reset in a primary chart

arguments.name: TESTS=
arguments.description: requests one or more tests for special causes, which are also known as runs tests, pattern tests, and Western Electric rules
specifies one point beyond Zone A (outside the control limits)
specifies nine points in a row in Zone C or beyond on one side of the central line
specifies six points in a row steadily increasing
specifies fourteen points in a row alternating up and down
specifies two out of three points in a row in Zone A or beyond
specifies four out of five points in a row in Zone B or beyond
specifies fifteen points in a row in Zone C on either or both sides of the central line
specifies eight points in a row on either or both sides of the central line with no points in Zone C

arguments.name: TESTS=
arguments.description: specifies customized tests for special causes for the primary control chart to search for k out of m points in a row in the interval (a,b)
arguments.help: TESTS=T(K=*k* M=*m* LOWER=*a* UPPER=*b* SCHEME=*scheme* CODE=*character* LABEL=*'label'* LEGEND=*'legend'*)

arguments.name: K=
arguments.description: specifies the number of points

arguments.name: M=
arguments.description: specifies the number of consecutive points

arguments.name: LOWER=
arguments.description: specifies the lower limit of interval (a,b)

arguments.name: UPPER=
arguments.description: specifies the upper limit of interval (a,b)

arguments.name: SCHEME=
arguments.description: specifies a one-sided scheme using (a,b)

arguments.name: SCHEME=
arguments.description: specifies a two-sided scheme using (a,b) union (-b,-a)

arguments.name: CODE=
arguments.description: specifies an identifier for test (A--H)

arguments.name: LABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a label for points if signal

arguments.name: LEGEND=
arguments.description: specifies a legend used with the TABLELEGEND option

arguments.name: TESTS=
arguments.description: specifies customized tests for special causes for the primary control chart to search for k points in a row increasing or decreasing

arguments.name: K=
arguments.description: specifies the number of points

arguments.name: DIR=
arguments.description: specifies an increasing pattern

arguments.name: DIR=
arguments.description: specifies a decreasing pattern

arguments.name: CODE=
arguments.description: specifies an identifier for test (A--H)

arguments.name: LABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a label for points if signal

arguments.name: LEGEND=
arguments.description: specifies a legend used with the TABLELEGEND option

arguments.name: TESTS=
arguments.description: specifies customized tests for special causes for the primary control chart to search for a statistically significant linear trend over a window of k points
arguments.help: TESTS=S(K=*k* CLEV=*α* FORM=*character* CODE=*character* LABEL=*'label'* LEGEND=*'legend'*)

arguments.name: K=
arguments.description: specifies the number of points in sliding window (k > 2)

arguments.name: CLEV=
arguments.description: specifies the Type I (false positive) error rate (0 < α ≤ 0.5)

arguments.name: FORM=
arguments.description: specifies the type of trend test (P=parametric, N=nonparametric)

arguments.name: CODE=
arguments.description: specifies an identifier for test (A--H)

arguments.name: LABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a label for points if signal

arguments.name: LEGEND=
arguments.description: specifies a legend used with the TABLELEGEND option

arguments.name: TOTPANELS=
arguments.description: specifies the total number of panels to be used to display the chart

arguments.name: TRENDVAR=
arguments.description: specifies a list of trend variables, one for each process listed in the chart statement

arguments.name: TYPE=
arguments.description: specifies the value of the _TYPE_ variable in the OUTLIMITS= data set, which in turn indicates whether certain parameter variables in this data set represent estimates or standard (known) values

arguments.name: UCLLABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a label for the upper control limit in the primary chart

arguments.name: USL=
arguments.description: provides upper specification limits used to compute capability indices

arguments.name: VAXIS=
arguments.description: specifies major tick mark values for the vertical axis of a primary chart

arguments.name: VAXIS2=
arguments.description: specifies major tick mark values for the vertical axis of a secondary chart

arguments.name: VFORMAT=
arguments.description: specifies a format to be used for displaying tick mark labels on the vertical axis of a primary chart

arguments.name: VFORMAT2=
arguments.description: specifies a format to be used for displaying tick mark labels on the vertical axis of a secondary chart

arguments.name: VOFFSET=
arguments.description: specifies the length of the offset at each end of the vertical axis

arguments.name: VREF=
arguments.description: draws reference lines perpendicular to the vertical axis on the primary chart

arguments.name: VREF2=
arguments.description: draws reference lines perpendicular to the vertical axis on the secondary chart

arguments.name: VREF2LABELS=
arguments.description: specifies labels for the reference lines requested by the VREF2= option

arguments.name: VREFLABELS=
arguments.description: specifies labels for the reference lines requested by the VREF= option

arguments.name: VREFLABPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the horizontal position of the VREFLABELS= and VREF2LABELS= labels
left-justifies in subplot area
right-justifies in subplot area
left-justifies in right margin

arguments.name: VZERO
arguments.description: forces the origin to be included in the vertical axis for a primary chart

arguments.name: VZERO2
arguments.description: forces the origin to be included in the vertical axis for a secondary chart

arguments.name: WESTGARD=
arguments.description: requests that one or more of the Westgard rules (tests for special causes) be applied
specifies one point in Zone A or beyond
specifies one point beyond Zone A (outside the control limits)
specifies two points in a row in Zone A or beyond on the same side of the central line
specifies at least one point in Zone A or beyond on each side of the central line
specifies four points in a row in Zone B or beyond on the same side of the central line
specifies ten points in a row on the same side of the central line

arguments.name: XSYMBOL=
arguments.description: specifies a label for the central line in an X-bar chart or a median chart
arguments.help: XSYMBOL=MBAR | MTIL | MU | MU0 | XBAR | XBAR2 | XBARPM | XBAR0 | XBAR0PM | '*label*'

arguments.name: MBAR
arguments.description: M bar

arguments.name: MTIL
arguments.description: M tilde

arguments.name: MU
arguments.description: μ

arguments.name: MU0
arguments.description: μ sub 0

arguments.name: XBAR
arguments.description: X bar

arguments.name: XBAR2
arguments.description: X double bar

arguments.name: XBARPM
arguments.description: X bar prime

arguments.name: XBAR0
arguments.description: X bar sub 0

arguments.name: XBAR0PM
arguments.description: X bar prime sub 0

arguments.name: YPCT1=
arguments.description: specifies a percent (ranging from 0 to 100) that determines the length of the vertical axis for the primary chart in proportion to the sum of the lengths of the vertical axes for the primary and secondary charts

arguments.name: ZEROSTD

arguments.name: ZONE2VALUES
arguments.description: labels R or s chart zones lines with their values

arguments.name: ZONELABELS
arguments.description: adds the labels A, B, and C to zone lines requested with the ZONES or ZONEVALUES options

arguments.name: ZONEVALPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the horizontal position of the ZONEVALUES= and ZONE2VALUES= labels
left-justifies in subplot area
right-justifies in subplot area
left-justifies in right margin

arguments.name: ZONEVALUES
arguments.description: labels the primary chart zones lines with their values

arguments.name: BLOCKREFTRANSPARENCY=
arguments.description: specifies the wall fill transparency for blocks and phases when transparency is used in ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: BOXTRANSPARENCY=
arguments.description: specifies the box fill transparency for box-and-whisker charts when transparency is used in ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: INFILLTRANSPARENCY=
arguments.description: specifies the control limit infill transparency when transparency is used in ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: MARKERDISPLAY=
arguments.description: specifies a subset of subgroups to be plotted with markers on a primary chart that is not an R or s chart

arguments.name: OOC
arguments.description: selects subgroups outside the control limits

arguments.name: UPPER
arguments.description: selects subgroups above the upper control limit

arguments.name: LOWER
arguments.description: selects subgroups below the lower control limit

arguments.name: RUNSTEST
arguments.description: selects subgroups that signal positive tests for special causes

arguments.name: MARKERDISPLAY2=
arguments.description: specifies a subset of subgroups to be plotted with markers on an R or s chart

arguments.name: OOC
arguments.description: selects subgroups outside the control limits

arguments.name: UPPER
arguments.description: selects subgroups above the upper control limit

arguments.name: LOWER
arguments.description: selects subgroups below the lower control limit

arguments.name: RUNSTEST
arguments.description: selects subgroups that signal positive tests for special causes

arguments.name: MARKERLABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a variable whose values provide labels for the subgroups that are plotted with markers on a primary chart that is not an R or s chart

arguments.name: MARKERLABEL2=
arguments.description: specifies a variable whose values provide labels for the subgroups that are plotted with markers on an R or s chart

arguments.name: MARKERMISSINGGROUP=
arguments.description: specifies whether subgroups with missing symbol-variable values are plotted with a unique marker

arguments.name: MARKERS
arguments.description: plots subgroup points with markers

arguments.name: NOBLOCKREF
arguments.description: suppresses block and phase reference lines from ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: NOBLOCKREFFILL
arguments.description: suppresses the block and phase wall fills from ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: NOBOXFILLLEGEND
arguments.description: suppresses the legend for the levels of a BOXFILL= or STARFILL= variable in ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: NOTRANSPARENCY
arguments.description: disables transparency in ODS Graphics output, so that all graph features are opaque

arguments.name: ODSFOOTNOTE=
arguments.description: adds a footnote to ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: ODSFOOTNOTE2=
arguments.description: adds a secondary footnote to ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: ODSLEGENDEXPAND
arguments.description: specifies that legend entries contain all levels observed in the data

arguments.name: ODSTITLE=
arguments.description: specifies a title for ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: ODSTITLE2=
arguments.description: specifies a secondary title for ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: OUTFILLTRANSPARENCY=
arguments.description: specifies the control limit outfill transparency when transparency is used in ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: OUTHIGHURL=

arguments.name: OVERLAY2URL=

arguments.description: specifies variables whose values are URLs to be associated with points on primary chart overlays

arguments.name: PHASEBOXLABELS
arguments.description: draws phase labels as titles along the top of phase boxes

arguments.name: PHASEPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the vertical position of the phase legend
places at top of chart, offset from axis frame
places at top of chart, immediately above axis frame
places at bottom of chart, immediately above horizontal axis
places at bottom of chart, below horizontal axis label

arguments.name: PHASEREFLEVEL=
arguments.description: enables you to associate phase reference lines (block reference lines) with either the innermost or the outermost level

arguments.name: QCSYMBOLS=
arguments.description: specifies which set of symbol markers is used to plot points on a control chart

arguments.name: SIMULATEQCFONT
arguments.description: draws the central line labels using a simulated software font rather than a hardware font

arguments.name: STARTRANSPARENCY=
arguments.description: specifies the star fill transparency when transparency is used in ODS Graphics output

arguments.name: URL=
arguments.description: specifies URLs as values of the specified character variable (or formatted values of a numeric variable) to associate with subgroup points on a primary control chart when ODS Graphics output is directed into HTML

arguments.name: URL2=
arguments.description: specifies URLs as values of the specified character variable (or formatted values of a numeric variable) to associate with subgroup points on a secondary control chart when ODS Graphics output is directed into HTML

arguments.name: ANNOTATE=
arguments.description: specifies an ANNOTATE= type data set that enhances a primary chart

arguments.name: ANNOTATE2=
arguments.description: specifies an ANNOTATE= type data set that enhances a secondary chart

arguments.name: BILEVEL
arguments.description: arranges the Shewhart chart in two levels (rather than the default of one level) so that twice as much data can be displayed on a page or screen

arguments.name: CAXIS=
arguments.description: specifies the color for the axes and tick marks

arguments.name: CBLOCKLAB=
arguments.description: specifies fill colors for the frames that enclose the block-variable labels in a block legend

arguments.name: CBLOCKVAR=
arguments.description: specifies variables whose values are colors for filling the background of the legend associated with block-variables

arguments.name: CCLIP=
arguments.description: specifies a color for the plotting symbol that is specified with the CLIPSYMBOL option to mark clipped points

arguments.name: CCONNECT=
arguments.description: specifies the color for the line segments connecting points on the chart

arguments.name: CCOVERLAY=
arguments.description: specifies the colors for the line segments connecting points on primary chart overlays

arguments.name: CCOVERLAY2=
arguments.description: specifies the colors for the line segments connecting points on secondary chart overlays

arguments.name: CFRAME=
arguments.description: specifies the colors for filling the rectangle enclosed by the axes and the frame

arguments.name: CGRID=
arguments.description: specifies the color for the grid requested by the ENDGRID or GRID option

arguments.name: CHREF=
arguments.description: specifies the color for the lines requested by the HREF= and HREF2= options

arguments.name: CLABEL=
arguments.description: specifies the color for labels produced by the ALLLABEL=, ALLLABEL2=, OUTLABEL=, and OUTLABEL2= options

arguments.name: CLIMITS=
arguments.description: specifies the color for the control limits, the central line, and the labels for these lines

arguments.name: CLIPLEGPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the position for the legend that indicates the number of clipped points when the CLIPFACTOR= option is used

arguments.name: CLIPSYMBOL=
arguments.description: specifies a plot symbol used to identify clipped points on the chart and in the legend when the CLIPFACTOR= option is used

arguments.name: CLIPSYMBOLHT=
arguments.description: specifies the height for the symbol marker used to identify clipped points on the chart when the CLIPFACTOR= option is used

arguments.name: CNEEDLES=
arguments.description: requests that points are to be connected to the central line with vertical line segments (needles) and specifies the color of the needles

arguments.name: COUTFILL=
arguments.description: specifies the fill color for the areas outside the control limits that lie between the connected points and the control limits and are bounded by connecting lines

arguments.name: COVERLAY=
arguments.description: specifies the colors used to plot primary chart overlay variables specified in the OVERLAY= list

arguments.name: COVERLAY2=
arguments.description: specifies the colors used to plot secondary chart overlay variables specified in the OVERLAY2= list

arguments.name: COVERLAYCLIP=
arguments.description: specifies the color used to plot clipped values on overlay plots when the CLIPFACTOR= option is used

arguments.name: CPHASELEG=
arguments.description: specifies a text color for the phase labels requested with the PHASELEGEND option

arguments.name: CSTARCIRCLES=
arguments.description: specifies a color for the circles requested with the STARCIRCLES= option

arguments.name: CSTARFILL=
arguments.description: specifies a color or colors for filling the interior of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: CSTARS=
arguments.description: specifies a color or colors for the outlines of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: CTESTLABBOX=
arguments.description: specifies the color for boxes enclosing labels for positive tests for special causes requested with the TESTLABBOX option

arguments.name: CTESTS=
arguments.description: specifies colors for labels indicating points where a test for special causes specified in the TESTS= option is positive

arguments.name: CTESTSYMBOL=
arguments.description: specifies the color of the symbol used to plot subgroups with positive tests for special causes

arguments.name: CTEXT=
arguments.description: specifies the color for tick mark values, axis labels, the sample size legend, and the control limit legend

arguments.name: CVREF=
arguments.description: specifies the color for reference lines requested by the VREF= and VREF2= options

arguments.name: CZONES=
arguments.description: requests lines marking zones A, B, and C for the tests for special causes

arguments.name: DESCRIPTION=
arguments.description: specifies a description, up to 256 characters long, for the GRSEG catalog entry for the primary chart

arguments.name: ENDGRID
arguments.description: a grid to the rightmost portion of the chart, beginning with the first labeled major tick mark position that follows the last plotted point

arguments.name: FONT=
arguments.description: specifies a software font for labels and legends

arguments.name: HEIGHT=
arguments.description: specifies the height (in vertical screen percent units) of the text for axis labels and legends

arguments.name: HMINOR=
arguments.description: specifies the number of (unlabeled) minor tick marks between each major tick mark on the horizontal axis

arguments.name: HTML=
arguments.description: specifies a variable whose values create links associated with subgroup points on a primary control chart when traditional graphics output is directed into HTML

arguments.name: HTML2=
arguments.description: specifies a variable whose values create links associated with subgroup points on a secondary control chart when traditional graphics output is directed into HTML

arguments.name: HTML_LEGEND=
arguments.description: specifies HTML links as values of the specified character variable (or formatted values of a numeric variable)

arguments.name: LABELANGLE=
arguments.description: specifies the angle at which labels requested with the ALLLABEL=, ALLLABEL2=, OUTLABEL=, and OUTLABEL2= options are drawn

arguments.name: LABELFONT=
arguments.description: specifies a software font for labels requested with the ALLLABEL=, ALLLABEL2=, OUTLABEL=, OUTLABEL2=, STARLABEL=, TESTLABEL=, and TESTLABELn= options

arguments.name: LABELHEIGHT=
arguments.description: specifies the height (in vertical percent screen units) for labels requested with the ALLLABEL=, ALLLABEL2=, OUTLABEL=, OUTLABEL2=, STARLABEL=, TESTLABEL=, and TESTLABELn= options

arguments.name: LENDGRID=
arguments.description: specifies the line type for the grid requested with the ENDGRID option

arguments.name: LGRID=
arguments.description: specifies the line type for the grid requested with the GRID option

arguments.name: LHREF=
arguments.description: specifies the line type for reference lines requested with the HREF= and HREF2= options

arguments.name: LLIMITS=
arguments.description: specifies the line type for control limits

arguments.name: LOVERLAY=
arguments.description: specifies line types for the line segments connecting points on primary chart overlays corresponding to the OVERLAY= list

arguments.name: LOVERLAY2=
arguments.description: specifies line types for the line segments connecting points on secondary chart overlays corresponding to the OVERLAY2= list

arguments.name: LSTARCIRCLES=
arguments.description: specifies one or more line types for the circles requested with the STARCIRCLES= option

arguments.name: LSTARS=
arguments.description: specifies the line types for the outlines of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: LTESTS=
arguments.description: specifies the line type for the line segments that connect patterns of points for which a test for special causes (requested with the TESTS= option) is positive

arguments.name: LVREF=
arguments.description: specifies the line type for reference lines requested by the VREF= and VREF2= options

arguments.name: LZONES=
arguments.description: specifies the line type for lines that delineate zones A, B, and C for standard tests requested with the TESTS= and/or TESTS2= options

arguments.name: NAME=
arguments.description: specifies the name of the GRSEG catalog entry for the primary chart, and the name of the graphics output file if one is created

arguments.name: NOFRAME
arguments.description: suppresses the default frame drawn around the chart

arguments.name: NOLIMITSFRAME
arguments.description: suppresses the default frame for the control limit information that is displayed across the top of the chart when multiple sets of control limits with distinct multiples of σ and nominal control limit sample sizes are read from a LIMITS= data set

arguments.name: NOPHASEFRAME
arguments.description: suppresses the default frame for the legend requested by the PHASELEGEND option

arguments.name: NOVANGLE
arguments.description: requests vertical axis labels that are strung out vertically

arguments.name: NOVLABEL
arguments.description: suppresses the label for the primary vertical axis

arguments.name: NOV2LABEL
arguments.description: suppresses the label for the secondary vertical axis

arguments.name: OUTHIGHHTML=
arguments.description: specifies a variable whose values create links to be associated with outlier points above the upper fence on a schematic box chart when traditional graphics output is directed into HTML

arguments.name: OUTLOWHTML=
arguments.description: specifies a variable whose values create links to be associated with outlier points below the lower fence on a schematic box chart when traditional graphics output is directed into HTML

arguments.name: OVERLAY2HTML=
arguments.description: specifies variables whose values create links to be associated with points on secondary chart overlays corresponding to the OVERLAY2= list

arguments.name: OVERLAY2SYM=
arguments.description: specifies symbols used to plot overlays on a secondary control chart corresponding to the OVERLAY2= list

arguments.name: OVERLAY2SYMHT=
arguments.description: specifies the heights of symbols used to plot overlays on a secondary control chart corresponding to the OVERLAY2= list

arguments.name: OVERLAYCLIPSYM=
arguments.description: specifies the symbol used to plot clipped values on overlay plots when the CLIPFACTOR= option is used

arguments.name: OVERLAYCLIPSYMHT=
arguments.description: specifies the height for the symbol used to plot clipped values on overlay plots when the CLIPFACTOR= option is used

arguments.name: OVERLAYHTML=
arguments.description: specifies variables whose values create links to be associated with points on primary chart overlays corresponding to the OVERLAY= list

arguments.name: OVERLAYSYM=
arguments.description: specifies symbols used to plot overlays on the primary control chart corresponding to the OVERLAY= list

arguments.name: OVERLAYSYMHT=
arguments.description: specifies the heights of symbols used to plot overlays on the primary control chart corresponding to the OVERLAY= list

arguments.name: TESTFONT=
arguments.description: specifies a software font for labels requested with the ALLLABEL=, ALLLABEL2=, OUTLABEL=, OUTLABEL2=, STARLABEL=, TESTLABEL=, and TESTLABELn= options

arguments.name: TESTHEIGHT=
arguments.description: specifies the height (in vertical percent screen units) for labels requested with the ALLLABEL=, ALLLABEL2=, OUTLABEL=, OUTLABEL2=, STARLABEL=, TESTLABEL=, and TESTLABELn= options

arguments.name: TESTSYMBOL=
arguments.description: specifies the symbol for plotting subgroups with positive tests for special causes

arguments.name: TESTSYMBOLHT=
arguments.description: specifies the height of the symbol used to plot subgroups with positive tests for special causes

arguments.name: TURNALL
arguments.description: turns the labels produced by the ALLLABEL=, ALLLABEL2=, OUTLABEL=, and OUTLABEL2= options so that they are strung out vertically

arguments.name: TURNHLABELS
arguments.description: turns the major tick mark labels for the horizontal (subgroup) axis so that they are strung out vertically

arguments.name: VMINOR=
arguments.description: specifies the number of minor tick marks between each major tick mark on the vertical axis

arguments.name: WAXIS=
arguments.description: specifies the width in pixels for the axis and frame lines

arguments.name: WGRID=
arguments.description: specifies the width in pixels for grid lines requested with the ENDGRID and GRID options

arguments.name: WLIMITS=
arguments.description: specifies the width in pixels for the control limits and central line

arguments.name: WNEEDLES=
arguments.description: specifies the width in pixels of needles connecting plotted points to the central line, as requested with the NEEDLES option

arguments.name: WOVERLAY=
arguments.description: specifies the widths in pixels for the line segments connecting points on primary chart overlay plots corresponding to the OVERLAY= list

arguments.name: WOVERLAY2=
arguments.description: specifies the widths in pixels for the line segments connecting points on secondary chart overlay plots corresponding to the OVERLAY2= list

arguments.name: WSTARCIRCLES=
arguments.description: specifies the width in pixels of the outline of circles requested by the STARCIRCLES= option

arguments.name: WSTARS=
arguments.description: specifies the width in pixels of the outline of stars requested by the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: WTESTS=
arguments.description: specifies the width in pixels of the line segments that connect patterns of points for which a test for special causes (requested with the TESTS= or TESTS2= option) is positive

arguments.name: WTREND=
arguments.description: specifies the width in pixels of the line segments that connect points on trend charts requested with the TRENDVAR= option

statements.name: MRCHART
statements.description: creates charts for subgroup medians and range
statements.help:
MRCHART  (processes) * subgroup-variable<(block-variables)><=symbol-variable | ='character'></ options>;                                              
process is the name of the variable containing the raw measurements, or the common prefix of the summary variables in the HISTORY= data set, or the value of the variable _VAR_ in the TABLE= data set                     
subgroup-variable is the variable that identifies subgroups in the data                     
block-variables are optional variables that group the data into blocks of consecutive subgroups                     
symbol-variable is an optional variable whose levels (unique values) determine the symbol marker that is used to plot the medians and ranges                     
character is a character that is used to plot the medians and ranges when you produce traditional graphics

arguments.name: ALLLABEL=
arguments.description: labels every point on the primary chart with the value plotted for that subgroup or with the value of variable in the input data set

arguments.name: ALLLABEL2=
arguments.description: labels every point on an R, s, or trend chart with the value plotted for that subgroup or with the value of variable in the input data set

arguments.name: ALLN
arguments.description: plots summary statistics for all subgroups, regardless of whether the subgroup sample size equals the nominal control limit sample size n specified by the LIMITN= option or the variable _LIMITN_ in the LIMITS= data set

arguments.name: ALPHA=
arguments.description: requests α probability limits instead of 3σ control limits

arguments.name: BLOCKLABELPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the position of a block-variable label in the block legend

arguments.name: BLOCKLABTYPE=
arguments.description: specifies how lengthy block variable values are treated when there is insufficient space to display them in the block legend

arguments.name: BLOCKPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the vertical position of the legend for the values of the block-variables

arguments.name: BLOCKREP
arguments.description: specifies that block variable values for all subgroups are to be displayed

arguments.name: BLOCKVAR=
arguments.description: specifies variables whose values are used to assign colors for filling the background of the legend associated with block variables

arguments.name: BOXES=
arguments.description: specifies a variable whose values are used to assign colors for the outlines of box-and-whiskers plots

arguments.name: BOXFILL=
arguments.description: specifies how box-and-whisker plots are filled with colors from the ODS style

arguments.name: CFRAMELAB=
arguments.description: specifies the color for filling rectangles that frame the point labels displayed with the ALLLABEL=, ALLLABEL2=, OUTLABEL=, and OUTLABEL2= options

arguments.name: CIINDICES
arguments.description: requests capability index confidence limits based on subgroup summary data, calculated using "effective degrees of freedom"

arguments.name: TYPE=
arguments.description: specifies the type of confidence limit

arguments.name: ALPHA=
arguments.description: specifies the default confidence level to compute confidence limits

arguments.name: CINFILL=
arguments.description: specifies the color for the area inside the upper and lower control limits

arguments.name: CLIPFACTOR=
arguments.description: requests clipping of extreme points on the control chart

arguments.name: CLIPLEGEND=
arguments.description: specifies the label for the legend that indicates the number of clipped points when the CLIPFACTOR= option is used

arguments.name: CLIPSUBCHAR=
arguments.description: specifies a substitution character (such as #) for the label provided with the CLIPLEGEND= option

arguments.name: COUT
arguments.description: specifies the color for the plotting symbols and the portions of connecting line segments that lie outside the control limits

arguments.name: CSTAROUT
arguments.description: specifies a color for those portions of the outlines of stars (requested with the STARVERTICES= option) that exceed the inner or outer circles

arguments.name: DISCRETE
arguments.description: specifies that numeric subgroup variable values be treated as discrete values, so that each tick value on the default subgroup axis corresponds to a unique subgroup variable value

arguments.name: EXCHART
arguments.description: creates a control chart only when exceptions occur, specifically, when the control limits are exceeded or when any of the tests requested with the TESTS= option or the TESTS2= option are positive

arguments.name: FRONTREF
arguments.description: draws reference lines specified with the HREF= and VREF= options in front of box-and-whiskers plots

arguments.name: GRID
arguments.description: adds a grid to the control chart

arguments.name: HAXIS=
arguments.description: specifies tick mark values for the horizontal (subgroup) axis

arguments.name: HOFFSET=
arguments.description: specifies the length of the offset at each end of the horizontal axis

arguments.name: HREF=
arguments.description: draws reference lines perpendicular to the horizontal (subgroup) axis on the primary chart

arguments.name: HREF2=
arguments.description: draws reference lines perpendicular to the horizontal (subgroup) axis on the secondary chart

arguments.name: HREF2DATA=
arguments.description: draws reference lines perpendicular to the horizontal (subgroup) axis on the secondary chart

arguments.name: HREF2LABELS=
arguments.description: specifies labels for the reference lines requested by the HREF2= option

arguments.name: HREFDATA=
arguments.description: draws reference lines perpendicular to the horizontal (subgroup) axis on the primary chart

arguments.name: HREFLABELS=
arguments.description: specifies labels for the reference lines requested by the HREF= option

arguments.name: HREFLABPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the vertical position of the HREFLABEL= and HREF2LABEL= labels
positions along top of subplot area
staggers from top to bottom of subplot area
positions along bottom of subplot area
staggers from bottom to top of subplot area

arguments.name: INDEPENDENTZONES
arguments.description: specifies that the widths of the zones requested with the ZONES option be computed independently above and below the center line of the chart, so that the width of each zone is one-third of the difference between the process mean and the control limit on its side of the chart

arguments.name: INTERVAL=
arguments.description: specifies the natural time interval between consecutive subgroup positions when a time, date, or datetime format is associated with a numeric subgroup variable
arguments.help: INTERVAL=DAY | DTDAY | HOUR | MINUTE | MONTH | QTR | SECOND

arguments.name: INTSTART=
arguments.description: specifies the starting value for a numeric horizontal axis, when a date, time, or datetime format is associated with the subgroup variable

arguments.name: LCLLABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a label for the lower control limit in the primary chart

arguments.name: LCLLABEL2=
arguments.description: specifies a label for the lower control limit in the secondary chart

arguments.name: LIMITN=
arguments.description: specifies either a fixed or varying nominal sample size for the control limits

arguments.name: LIMLABSUBCHAR=
arguments.description: specifies a substitution character (such as #) for labels provided as quoted strings with the LCLLABEL=, LCLLABEL2=, UCLLABEL=, UCLLABEL2=, CSYMBOL=, NPSYMBOL=, PSYMBOL=, RSYMBOL=, SSYMBOL=, USYMBOL=, and XSYMBOL= options

arguments.name: LSL=
arguments.description: provides lower specification limits used to compute capability indices

arguments.name: MAXPANELS=
arguments.description: specifies the maximum number of pages or screens for a chart

arguments.name: MEDCENTRAL=
arguments.description: identifies a method for estimating the process mean μ, which is represented by the central line on a median chart

arguments.name: AVGMEAN
arguments.description: uses the average of subgroup means

arguments.name: AVGMED
arguments.description: uses the average of subgroup medians

arguments.name: MEDMED
arguments.description: uses the median of subgroup medians

arguments.name: MISSBREAK
arguments.description: determines how subgroups are formed when observations are read from a DATA= data set and a character subgroup-variable is provided

arguments.name: MU0=
arguments.description: specifies a known (standard) value μ0 for the process mean μ

arguments.name: NDECIMAL=
arguments.description: specifies the number of decimal digits in the default labels for the control limits and the central line in the primary chart

arguments.name: NDECIMAL2=
arguments.description: specifies the number of decimal digits in the default labels for the control limits and central line in a secondary chart

arguments.name: NEEDLES
arguments.description: connects plotted points to the central line with vertical line segments (needles)

arguments.name: NMARKERS
arguments.description: identifies a plotted subgroup summary statistic with a special symbol marker (character) when the corresponding subgroup sample size is not equal to the nominal control limit sample size n

arguments.name: NO3SIGMACHECK
arguments.description: suppresses the check for 3σ limits when tests for special causes are requested

arguments.name: NOBYREF
arguments.description: specifies that the reference line information in an HREF=, HREF2=, VREF=, or VREF2= data set is to be applied uniformly to charts created for all the BY groups in the input data set (DATA=, HISTORY=, or TABLE=)

arguments.name: NOCHART
arguments.description: suppresses the creation of the chart

arguments.name: NOCHART2
arguments.description: suppresses the creation of a secondary chart

arguments.name: NOCONNECT
arguments.description: suppresses line segments that connect points on the chart

arguments.name: NOCTL
arguments.description: suppresses the display of the central line in a primary chart

arguments.name: NOCTL2
arguments.description: suppresses the display of the central line in a secondary chart

arguments.name: NOHLABEL
arguments.description: suppresses the label for the horizontal (subgroup) axis

arguments.name: NOLCL
arguments.description: suppresses the display of the lower control limit in a primary chart

arguments.name: NOLCL2
arguments.description: suppresses the drawing of the lower control limit in a secondary chart

arguments.name: NOLEGEND
arguments.description: suppresses the default legend for subgroup sample sizes, which appears by default below the chart

arguments.name: NOLIMIT0
arguments.description: suppresses the display of a fixed lower control limit if and only if the value of the limit is zero

arguments.name: NOLIMITLABEL
arguments.description: suppresses the default labels for the control limits and central lines

arguments.name: NOLIMITS
arguments.description: suppresses the display of control limits

arguments.name: NOLIMITSLEGEND
arguments.description: suppresses the legend for the control limits, which appears by default in the upper right corner of the chart

arguments.name: NOOVERLAYLEGEND
arguments.description: suppresses the legend for overlay variables which is displayed by default when the OVERLAY= or OVERLAY2= option is specified

arguments.name: NOREADLIMITS
arguments.description: specifies that the control limits for each process listed in the chart statement not be read from the LIMITS= data set specified in the PROC SHEWHART statement

arguments.name: NOTESTACROSS
arguments.description: specifies that tests for special causes requested with the TESTS= or TESTS2= options not be applied across the boundaries of phases (blocks of consecutive subgroups) determined by the READPHASES= option and the variable _PHASE_ in the input data set

arguments.name: NOTICKREP
arguments.description: applies to character-valued subgroup-variables and specifies that only the first occurrence of repeated, adjacent subgroup values is to be labeled on the horizontal axis

arguments.name: NOTRUNC
arguments.description: overrides the vertical axis truncation at zero, which is applied by default to c charts, moving range charts, np charts, p charts, R charts, s charts, and u charts

arguments.name: NOUCL
arguments.description: suppresses the display of the upper control limit in a primary chart

arguments.name: NOUCL2
arguments.description: suppresses the display of the upper control limit in a secondary chart

arguments.name: NPANELPOS=
arguments.description: specifies the number of subgroup positions per panel on each chart

arguments.name: OUTFILL
arguments.description: fills the areas outside the control limits that lie between the connected points and the control limits and are bounded by connecting lines

arguments.name: OUTHISTORY=
arguments.description: creates an output data set that contains the subgroup summary statistics

arguments.name: OUTINDEX=
arguments.description: specifies the value of the _INDEX_ variable in the OUTLIMITS= output data set

arguments.name: OUTLABEL=
arguments.description: labels each point that falls outside the control limits on the primary chart with the value plotted for that subgroup or with the value of variable in the input data set

arguments.name: OUTLABEL2=
arguments.description: labels each point that falls outside the control limits on an R or s chart with the value plotted for that subgroup or with the value of variable in the input data set

arguments.name: OUTLIMITS=
arguments.description: creates an output data set that saves the control limits

arguments.name: OUTPHASE=
arguments.description: specifies the value of the _PHASE_ variable in the OUTHISTORY= data set

arguments.name: OUTTABLE=
arguments.description: creates an output SAS data set that saves the information plotted on the chart, including the subgroup variable values and their corresponding summary statistics and control limits

arguments.name: OVERLAY=
arguments.description: specifies variables to be overlaid on the primary control chart

arguments.name: OVERLAY2=
arguments.description: specifies variables to be overlaid on a secondary control chart

arguments.name: OVERLAY2ID=
arguments.description: specifies variables whose formatted values are used to label points on secondary chart overlays

arguments.name: OVERLAYID=
arguments.description: specifies variables whose formatted values are used to label points on primary chart overlays

arguments.name: OVERLAYLEGLAB=
arguments.description: specifies the label displayed to the left of the legend for overlays requested with the OVERLAY= or OVERLAY2= option

arguments.name: PAGENUM=
arguments.description: specifies the form of the label used for pagination; for example, 'Page #' or 'Page # of #'

arguments.name: PAGENUMPOS=
arguments.description: specifies where to position the page number requested with the PAGENUM= option
arguments.help: PAGENUMPOS=TL | TR | BL | BR | TL100 | TR100 | BL0 | BR0

arguments.name: TL
arguments.description: specifies the top left

arguments.name: TR
arguments.description: specifies the top right

arguments.name: BL
arguments.description: specifies the bottom left

arguments.name: BR
arguments.description: specifies the bottom right

arguments.name: TL100
arguments.description: specifies the very top left

arguments.name: TR100
arguments.description: specifies the very top right

arguments.name: BL0
arguments.description: specifies the very bottom left

arguments.name: BR0
arguments.description: specifies the very bottom right

arguments.name: PHASEBREAK
arguments.description: specifies that the last point in a phase (defined as a block of consecutive subgroups with the same value of the _PHASE_ variable) is not to be connected to the first point in the next phase

arguments.name: PHASELABTYPE=
arguments.description: specifies how lengthy _PHASE_ variable values are displayed when there is insufficient space in the legend requested with the PHASELEGEND option

arguments.name: PHASELEGEND
arguments.description: identifies the phases requested with the READPHASES= option in a legend across the top of the chart

arguments.name: PHASELIMITS
arguments.description: specifies that the control limits and center line be labeled for each phase specified with the READPHASES= option, providing the limits are constant within that phase

arguments.name: PHASEREF
arguments.description: delineates the phases specified with the READPHASES= option with reference lines drawn vertically

arguments.name: PHASEVARLABEL
arguments.description: displays the label associated with the variable _PHASE_ above the phase values in the phase legend

arguments.name: PHASEVALSEP
arguments.description: displays vertical lines separating phase values in the phase legend

arguments.name: RANGES
arguments.description: estimates the process standard deviation for a boxplot using subgroup ranges

arguments.name: READALPHA
arguments.description: specifies that the variable _ALPHA_, rather than the variable _SIGMAS_, be read from a LIMITS= data set when both variables are available in the data set

arguments.name: READINDEX=
arguments.description: reads one or more sets of control limits from a LIMITS= data set (specified in the PROC SHEWHART statement) for each process listed in the chart statement

arguments.name: READPHASES=
arguments.description: selects blocks of consecutive observations to be read from the input data set

arguments.name: REPEAT
arguments.description: specifies that the horizontal axis of a chart that spans multiple pages be arranged so that the last subgroup position on a page is repeated as the first subgroup position on the next page

arguments.name: SEPARATE
arguments.description: displays primary and secondary charts on separate screens or pages

arguments.name: SIGMA0=
arguments.description: specifies a known (standard) value σ0 for the process standard deviation σ

arguments.name: SIGMAS=
arguments.description: specifies the width of the control limits in terms of the multiple k of the standard error of the median and range statistic plotted on the chart

arguments.name: SKIPHLABELS=
arguments.description: specifies the number n of consecutive tick mark labels, beginning with the second tick mark label, that are thinned (not displayed) on the horizontal (subgroup) axis

arguments.name: SMETHOD=
arguments.description: specifies a method for estimating the process standard deviation, σ

arguments.name: NOWEIGHT
arguments.description: estimates σ as an unweighted average of unbiased subgroup estimates of σ

arguments.name: MVLUE
arguments.description: calculates a minimum variance linear unbiased estimate for σ

arguments.name: SPLIT=
arguments.description: specifies a special character that is inserted into the label of a process variable or summary statistic variable and whose purpose is to split the label into two parts

arguments.name: STARBDRADIUS=
arguments.description: specifies the radius of an imaginary circle that is the outer bound for vertices of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARCIRCLES=
arguments.description: specifies reference circles that are superimposed on the stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARINRADIUS=
arguments.description: specifies the inner radius of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARFILL=
arguments.description: specifies colors for filling the interior of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARLABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a method for labeling the vertices of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: ALL
arguments.description: labels all vertices of all stars

arguments.name: FIRST
arguments.description: labels all vertices of the leftmost star

arguments.name: HIGH
arguments.description: labels only vertices that lie outside the outer circle

arguments.name: LOW
arguments.description: labels only vertices that lie inside the inner circle

arguments.name: OUT
arguments.description: labels only vertices that lie inside the inner circle or outside the outer circle

arguments.name: STARLEGEND=
arguments.description: specifies the style of the legend used to identify the vertices of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: CLOCK
arguments.description: identifies the vertex variables by their positions on the clock (starting with 12:00)

arguments.name: CLOCK0
arguments.description: identifies the vertex variables by their positions on the clock (starting with 0:00)

arguments.name: DEGREES
arguments.description: identifies the vertex variables by angles in degrees, with 0 degrees corresponding to 12 o'clock

arguments.name: NONE
arguments.description: suppresses the legend

arguments.name: STARLEGENDLAB=
arguments.description: specifies the label displayed to the left of the legend for stars requested with the STARLEGEND= option

arguments.name: STARS=
arguments.description: specifies colors for the outlines of stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARSPECS=
arguments.description: specifies the method used to standardize the star vertex variables listed with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: STARSTART=
arguments.description: specifies the vertex angle for the first variable in the STARVERTICES= list

arguments.name: STARTYPE=
arguments.description: specifies the style of the stars requested with the STARVERTICES= option

arguments.name: CORONA
arguments.description: specifies a polygon with star-vertices emanating from the inner circle

arguments.name: POLYGON
arguments.description: specifies a closed polygon

arguments.name: RADIAL
arguments.description: draws rays emanating from the center

arguments.name: SPOKE
arguments.description: draws rays emanating from the inner circle

arguments.name: WEDGE
arguments.description: specifies a closed polygon with rays from the center to each vertex

arguments.name: STARVERTICES=
arguments.description: superimposes a star (polygon) at each point on the primary chart

arguments.name: SYMBOLLEGEND=
arguments.description: controls the legend for the levels of a symbol-variable

arguments.name: SYMBOLORDER=
arguments.description: specifies the order in which symbols are assigned for levels of symbol-variable

arguments.name: TABLES
arguments.description: creates a basic table of the subgroup values, the subgroup sample sizes, the subgroup summary statistics, and the upper and lower control limits

arguments.name: TABLEALL
arguments.description: tabulates all the information about the control chart

arguments.name: TABLECENTRAL
arguments.description: augments the basic table created by the TABLES option with columns for the values of the central lines

arguments.name: TABLEID
arguments.description: augments the basic table created by the TABLES option with a column for each of the ID variables

arguments.name: TABLELEGEND
arguments.description: adds a legend to the basic table created by the TABLE option

arguments.name: TABLEOUTLIM
arguments.description: augments the basic table created by the TABLE option with columns indicating which control limits (if any) are exceeded

arguments.name: TABLETESTS
arguments.description: augments the basic table created by the TABLES option with a column that indicates which of the tests for special causes (requested with the TESTS= option) are positive

arguments.name: TARGET=
arguments.description: provides target values used to compute the capability index Cpm, which is saved in the OUTLIMITS= data set

arguments.name: TEST2RESET=
arguments.description: enables tests for special causes to be reset in a secondary chart

arguments.name: TEST2RUN=
arguments.description: specifies the length of the pattern for Test 2 requested with the TESTS= and TESTS2= options
specifies a run-length of 7 in a row on one side of the central line
specifies a run-length of 8 in a row on one side of the central line
specifies a run-length of 9 in a row on one side of the central line
specifies at least 10 out of 11 in a row on one side of the central line
specifies at least 12 out of 14 in a row on one side of the central line
specifies at least 16 out of 20 in a row on one side of the central line

arguments.name: TEST3RUN=
arguments.description: specifies the length of the pattern for Test 3 requested with the TESTS= and TESTS2= options
searches for a pattern of steadily increasing or decreasing values with length at least 6
searches for a pattern of steadily increasing or decreasing values with length at least 7
searches for a pattern of steadily increasing or decreasing values with length at least 8

arguments.name: TESTACROSS
arguments.description: specifies that tests for special causes requested with the TESTS= or TESTS2= options be applied without regard to phases (blocks of consecutive subgroups) determined by the READPHASES= option and the variable _PHASE_ in the input data set

arguments.name: TESTLABBOX
arguments.description: requests that labels for subgroups with positive tests for special causes are positioned so they do not overlap

arguments.name: TESTLABEL=
arguments.description: provides labels for points at which one of the tests for special causes (requested with the TESTS= or TESTS2= option) is positive

arguments.name: TESTLABEL=
arguments.description: specifies a label for points at which the test for special causes requested with the index n in a TESTS= or TESTS2= list is positive

arguments.name: TESTNMETHOD=
arguments.description: applies the tests for special causes requested with the TESTS= and TESTS2= options to standardized test statistics when the subgroup sample sizes are not constant

arguments.name: TESTOVERLAP
arguments.description: applies tests for special causes (requested with the TESTS= or TESTS2= option) to overlapping patterns of points

arguments.name: TESTRESET=
arguments.description: enables tests for special causes to be reset in a primary chart

arguments.name: TESTS=
arguments.description: requests one or more tests for special causes, which are also known as runs tests, pattern tests, and Western Electric rules
specifies one point beyond Zone A (outside the control limits)
specifies nine points in a row in Zone C or beyond on one side of the central line
specifies six points in a row steadily increasing
specifies fourteen points in a row alternating up and down
specifies two out of three points in a row in Zone A or beyond
specifies four out of five points in a row in Zone B or beyond
specifies fifteen points in a row in Zone C on either or both sides of the central line
specifies eight points in a row on either or both sides of the central line with no points in Zone C

arguments.name: TESTS=
arguments.description: specifies customized tests for special causes for the primary control chart to search for k out of m points in a row in the interval (a,b)
arguments.help: TESTS=T(K=*k* M=*m* LOWER=*a* UPPER=*b* SCHEME=*scheme* CODE=*character* LABEL=*'label'* LEGEND=*'legend'*)

arguments.name: K=
arguments.description: specifies the number of points

arguments.name: M=
arguments.description: specifies the number of consecutive points

arguments.name: LOWER=
arguments.description: specifies the lower limit of interval (a,b)
